skill.name: image-to-code
skill.description: Guides AI agents through an image-first workflow for high-fidelity website design and frontend implementation.
skill.summary: Create polished websites by generating visual references before implementation.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported shell execution, cryptography, sensitive file, and reconnaissance patterns, but review showed these are Markdown instructions and design vocabulary. No executable code, network access, credential access, filesystem access, data exfiltration, or prompt-injection attempt was found in the single SKILL.md file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code Misread As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 76-92 contain inline Markdown code spans that describe numeric design scales, not shell commands or Ruby backtick execution. Confidence: 0.97. Confidence reasoning: The flagged lines are plain text scale labels with no command names, arguments, interpolation, or execution context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The syntax is Markdown formatting around prose. There is no executable runtime or command invocation in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Vocabulary Misread As Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines use words such as design, system, generate, solid, and extract in website-design instructions, not cryptographic APIs or algorithms. Confidence: 0.95. Confidence reasoning: Review found no hashing, encryption, cipher, certificate, or password-handling code at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a Markdown prompt guide and contains no cryptographic operations. The matched terms are used in design-system prose.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Website Image System Heading Misread As Windows SAM
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 666 is the heading for the website image system rule, not a reference to the Windows SAM database. Confidence: 0.99. Confidence reasoning: The surrounding lines discuss website imagery and visual systems, with no Windows paths, registry access, credential stores, or operating-system commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line is a Markdown heading about website image systems. It has no sensitive file access semantics.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Layout Language Misread As Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines discuss layout grids, visual density, spacing, labels, and section structure, not host, system, or network enumeration. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: Review found no commands, API calls, IP ranges, port scans, host discovery, or environment inspection at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are design guidance in Markdown. They do not contain reconnaissance commands, network primitives, or host discovery behavior.

content.value_statement: Visual website builds often drift from the intended design. This skill makes Claude, Codex, and Claude Code generate and analyze visual references before coding.
actual_capabilities: Requires image generation before implementation for visually important website tasks.
Guides section-level image generation for clearer design references.
Extracts typography, spacing, palette, imagery, and layout details from generated images.
Directs frontend implementation to match the analyzed visual reference.
Discourages generic layouts, unreadable design boards, nested cards, and excessive decorative labels.
Provides final quality checks for design fidelity and responsive presentation.
limitations: It depends on image generation and image inspection being available.
It does not include executable code, templates, or automation scripts.
It is focused on websites, landing pages, product pages, and portfolio pages.
Generated image text may need regeneration when it is too small or unclear.

use_cases.title: Build A Premium Landing Page
use_cases.description: Generate section references, extract the design system, and implement a polished landing page with strong visual fidelity.

use_cases.title: Redesign A Marketing Site
use_cases.description: Create fresh visual directions and convert the selected direction into structured frontend sections.

use_cases.title: Translate Concepts Into Frontend
use_cases.description: Use generated images as implementation references for typography, spacing, color, and responsive layout decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Simple Hero
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an image-first hero section for a modern product website, analyze the visual details, then implement the matching frontend.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one clear section

prompt_templates.title: Design A Landing Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a four-section landing page using separate visual references for each section. Analyze the design system before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Redesign An Existing Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Redesign this website with stronger art direction. Generate new section references, preserve the brand intent, then build the frontend.

prompt_templates.title: Produce A Detailed Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate section images and detail views for a premium editorial website. Extract typography, spacing, palette, imagery, and component rules before coding.

output_examples.input: Create a premium SaaS landing page for an analytics product.
output: A generated hero reference with clear typography, product imagery, and restrained supporting content.
A design analysis covering palette, spacing, layout rhythm, and button treatment.
A frontend implementation that follows the visual reference instead of using a generic layout.

output_examples.input: Redesign a portfolio homepage with stronger editorial direction.
output: Separate visual references for the hero, project grid, about section, and contact section.
A concise design-system extraction for type scale, image treatment, and section spacing.
A responsive implementation plan that preserves the generated visual direction.

best_practices: Generate one clear image for each important section before implementation.
Regenerate references when text, buttons, or spacing cannot be inspected clearly.
Use the extracted design system as the implementation source of truth.
anti_patterns: Skipping image generation and coding directly for visual website tasks.
Using one compressed design board for many sections.
Replacing the visual reference with a generic card-heavy layout.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create visually guided websites, landing pages, product pages, portfolio sites, and marketing pages.

faq.question: Does it generate production code by itself?
faq.answer: No. It is a prompt workflow that guides an AI agent through visual generation, analysis, and implementation.

faq.question: Why does it require image generation first?
faq.answer: The generated image becomes the visual source of truth for typography, spacing, layout, and art direction.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Is this skill useful for backend projects?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for visually important frontend and website work.

faq.question: What should users provide for best results?
faq.answer: Users should provide the site goal, brand direction, target sections, content needs, and any existing visual constraints.
